The court advises Ms. Friedeberg that it seems premature to discuss issues with surcharges and that although the instant petition appears to be problematic, the court is considering accepting and perhaps approving the petition, her clients can file a petition for surcharge if they'd like to. Ms. Darrah reports that the estate is ready to close, and that the next petition should be a petition for final distribution which would include an accounting for three years, which can likely be filed by this October. Ms. Friedeberg reports of attempts to return the firearms and of advising opposing counsel of the difficulties to transfer firearms. Ms. Friedeberg, Ms. Darrah, and the court discuss the firearms issue and advises that it is her understanding that her client does not necessarily want the firearms returned.

Off Calendar Jan-29-2020 Continued To Feb-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeanne Darrah for Antonio White (Petitioner/Administrator).
Jonathan Maddison for Antonio White (Beneficiary).
Kendall Hillman appeared by telephone for Christopher Henry, Jr.
Trisha Friedeberg for and with Natalie Zieber (appeared by telephone) and for and with Kathy White (appeared by telephone).

The Court stated that it has not received a corrected verification that had been promised at the last calling. Ms. Darrah apologized to the Court, stated that she misunderstood, that she will file it, that oppositions and responses have been filed, and that the matter had been continued for discovery, but no discovery has been done. Mr. Maddison stated that Ms. Friedeberg sent an e-mail yesterday indicating that no discovery was needed. Ms. Friedeberg stated that information was provided informally. Mr. Hillman stated that discovery may not be needed, but he has not been provided with documents that were requested informally. Ms. Darrah questions what documents are being requested by Mr. Hillman. Mr. Hillman stated that he would like to be made party to the information provided to Ms. Friedeberg, that he will communicate with Counsel and that if the information provided is not fully sufficient, he will propound discovery. Ms. Friedeberg stated that her Clients are prepared to submit on the pleadings and that she does not think mediation would be helpful. Mr. Hillman stated that he is not prepared to submit on the pleadings and that if the information that is provided is not sufficient, he may request an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Maddison stated that the information that was provided to Ms. Friedeberg is that the property is in good condition. The Court admonished the Parties for not communicating with Mr. Hillman and stated that if there is any discovery, it needs to happen now. The Second Report on Status of Administration is under submission. If the Court does not receive the corrected verification, the matter will be restored back to the calendar. The Petition for Performance of Executory Contract, etc. is continued to February 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Mr. Hillman's Client to catch up. The Court noted that it is the right of the Parties to request an evidentiary hearing and encouraged the Parties to work on avoiding an evidentiary hearing. The Parties are to file a joint status report by February 19, 2020. The Petition for Performance of Executory Contract, etc. is continued to January 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
